Brendan J. Tuohy of K&L Gates (415-249-1077), appearing for defendant / responding party Crane Co.
(Refer to Sign-in Sheet for all other appearances.)

RULING:
PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR ORDER GRANTING PREFERENCE STATUS PURSUANT TO C.C.P. 36(D) IN SETTING CASE FOR TRIAL; MAINTAINING CURRENT TRIAL DATE; AND EXTENDING DISCOVERY CUTOFF

No argument made and tentative ruling adopted: Plaintiff's motion to maintain the current trial date with preference status pursuant to C.C.P. 36(d) is denied without prejudice. The Court will not consider a doctor declaration based on an exam of plaintiff that occurred four months prior to this hearing. Even were Dr. Wai's declaration fully considered, plaintiff failed to meet the requirements of C.C.P. 36(d) because he failed to submit clear and convincing medical documentation that raises substantial medical doubt of his survival beyond six months and that satisfies the court that the interests of justice will be served by granting preference. One order signed for both motions. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Reporter: Holly Sayers of AIKEN WELCH, CSR #13678 (Tel: 510-451-1580) (503/TLJ)
